description: A square solid-blue (ICE corporate blue) quotation graphic with no photograph. A large light-blue/gray opening curly quotation mark sits at the upper left. The main text is set in a large white serif typeface reading: 'Despite ... false information ... about the brave men and women who work for ICE, they continue to ... put their own lives at risk to make our communities safer.' Below the quote, an attribution in smaller white type reads '-BRET BRADFORD' with 'ICE Houston Field Office Director' beneath it. The lower-right corner carries the DHS seal and the bold white 'ICE' wordmark. This is a defensive/morale messaging graphic: the propaganda technique frames ICE officers as heroic and self-sacrificing while dismissing criticism as 'false information,' aimed at supportive public audiences and at boosting agency image amid scrutiny.
notable_text: Despite ... false information ... about the brave men and women who work for ICE, they continue to ... put their own lives at risk to make our communities safer. -BRET BRADFORD, ICE Houston Field Office Director
